practice.name: Problem 6 (2019 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: For how many of the following types of quadrilaterals does there exist a point in the plane that is equidistant from all four vertices? a square, a rectangle (not square), a rhombus (not square), a parallelogram (not rectangle/rhombus), an isosceles trapezoid (not parallelogram)

practice.name: Problem 12 (1991 AIME)
practice.statement: A rhombus is inscribed in a rectangle with side lengths 7 and 24 so that the vertices are interior points on each side. The longer diagonal is parallel to the longer side. If the perimeter is m√n, find m + n.

practice.name: Problem 4 (2019 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Quadrilateral ABCD is a rhombus with perimeter 52 meters. The length of diagonal AC is 24 meters. What is the area, in square meters, of rhombus ABCD?

practice.name: Problem 21 (2003 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The area of trapezoid $ABCD$ is $164\text{ cm}^2$. The altitude is $8\text{ cm}$, $AB$ is $10\text{ cm}$, and $CD$ is $17\text{ cm}$. What is $BC$, in centimeters? [See figure in original PDF]
